heading: Appearance of attorneys for Secretary, deputy commissioner, or Board
label: 921a Appearance of attorneys for Secretary, deputy commissioner, or Board
text: Attorneys appointed by the Secretary shall represent the Secretary, the deputy commissioner, or the Board in any court proceedings under section 21 [] or other provisions of this Act except for proceedings in the Supreme Court of the United States.33 U.S.C. 9211See References in Text note below.1
notes: References in TextThis Act, referred to in text, probably should have been a reference to , , known as the Longshore and Harbor Workers’ Compensation Act, which is classified generally to this chapter. This section was not enacted as part of that Act, see Codification note below. For complete classification of act , to the Code, see and Tables.act Mar. 4, 1927, ch. 50944 Stat. 1424section 901 of this titleMar. 4, 1927
CodificationSection was not enacted as part of the Longshore and Harbor Workers’ Compensation Act which comprises this chapter.

Amendments1972—, which directed the general amendment of “[s]ection 21a of the Act”, meaning section 21a of act , was executed to this section, which is , to reflect the probable intent of Congress. Act , does not contain a section 21a. Prior to amendment, section required the United States attorney in the judicial district in which a case was pending to appear as attorney or counsel on behalf of the Secretary of Labor or his deputy commissioner when either was a party to the case or interested, and to represent such Secretary or deputy in any court in which such case could be carried on appeal.Pub. L. 92–576act May 4, 1928, ch. 502Mar. 4, 1927Mar. 4, 1927

Change of NameAct , eff. , substituted “United States attorney” for “district attorney of the United States”. See , Judiciary and Judicial Procedure, and Historical and Revision note thereunder.June 25, 1948Sept. 1, 1948section 541 of Title 28
Effective Date of 1972 AmendmentAmendment by effective 30 days after , see , set out as a note under .Pub. L. 92–576section 22 of Pub. L. 92–576section 902 of this titleOct. 27, 1972
